conclusion: Underwriter is SiriusPoint Specialty Insurance Corporation. Plan Administrator is International Medical Group (IMG). Master Policy effective April 29, 2026.
exact_language: SiriusPoint Specialty Insurance Corporation (the Company) promises and agrees to provide the Insured Person with the benefits described in the Master Policy... The Master Policy is effective as of April 29, 2026

conclusion: Period of Coverage limit: $1M/$2M/$5M/$8M (age ≤64); $1M (age 65–69); $100K (age 70–79); $20K (age 80+). Coverage period 5 days to 12 months. Significantly higher limits than Patriot America Plus.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Coverage Limit / Maximum Amount

conclusion: Deductible options: $0, $100, $250, $500, $1,000, $2,500, $5,000, $10,000, or $25,000 per insured person per Period of Coverage. Wider range than Patriot America Plus.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Deductible for Eligible Medical Expenses
exact_language: Deductible $0, $100, $250, $500, $1,000, $2,500, $5,000, $10,000 or $25,000 per Insured Person, as indicated on the Declaration

conclusion: Plan pays 100% everywhere — in-network, out-of-network, and international. Insured pays 0%. Out-of-pocket maximum $0. No PPO penalty unlike Patriot America Plus.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Coinsurance for Eligible Medical Expenses
exact_language: Coinsurance Plan pays 100% • In addition to Deductible Insured pays 0% Out of Pocket Maximum $0

conclusion: Pre-existing conditions are EXCLUDED. Charges directly or indirectly from any Pre-existing Condition are not covered.
exact_language: Charges resulting directly or indirectly from or relating to any Pre-existing Condition are excluded from coverage under this insurance.

benefit: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions — Non-US Citizens
conclusion: For NON-US citizens (Indian nationals on B-1/B-2): Acute Onset covered up to $1,000,000 for age 69 and under. Insured Person must be under 70 years of age.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions (Non-United States citizens)
exact_language: Non-United States citizens: • Age 69 and under: • Maximum Limit: $1,000,000

benefit: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions — US Citizens
conclusion: For US citizens: Age ≤64 without Primary Health Plan: $20,000 limit. Age ≤64 WITH Primary Health Plan: $1,000,000 limit. Age 65–69: $2,500 limit. Not relevant for Indian B-1/B-2 visitors but documented for completeness.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions (United States citizens)
exact_language: United States citizens: • Age 64 and under without a Primary Health Plan: • Maximum Limit: $20,000 / • Age 64 and under with a Primary Health Plan: • Maximum Limit: $1,000,000 / • Age 65 through age 69: • Maximum Limit: $2,500

conclusion: Same conditions as Patriot America Plus: Treatment within 24 hours, under age 70, not traveling against medical advice, not traveling to seek treatment, pre-existing condition stabilized ≥30 days prior to Effective Date, must be outside Country of Residence.
section: I. ACUTE ONSET OF PRE-EXISTING CONDITIONS — CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS
exact_language: (a) Treatment must be obtained within twenty-four (24) hours of the sudden and Unexpected outbreak or reoccurrence (b) the Insured Person must be under seventy (70) years of age ... (f) the Pre-existing Condition must have been stabilized for at least thirty (30) days prior to the Effective Date without change in Treatment (g) the Insured Person must be traveling outside their Country of Residence.

conclusion: ER for Injury: NOT subject to ER Deductible. Plan pays 100%.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Hospital Emergency Room: United States
exact_language: Hospital Emergency Room: United States • Injury: Not subject to Emergency Room Deductible 100%

conclusion: ER for Illness: $250 additional Deductible per ER visit if Treatment does NOT result in direct Hospital admission. Plan pays 100% otherwise.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Hospital Emergency Room: United States
exact_language: • Illness: Subject to a $250 Deductible for each Emergency Room visit for Treatment that does not result in a direct Hospital admission 100%

conclusion: Inpatient hospitalization covered at average semi-private room rate including nursing and ancillary services. Plan pays 100%.
exact_language: Hospitalization / Room & Board • Average semi-private room rate • Includes nursing services, miscellaneous and Ancillary services 100%

conclusion: ICU covered at 100%.
exact_language: Intensive Care 100%

conclusion: Surgery covered at 100%. Reconstructive surgery covered when incidental to covered surgery. Assistant surgeon at 20% of primary surgeon's eligible fee.
exact_language: Surgery 100% / Reconstructive Surgery • Surgery is incidental to and follows Surgery that was covered under the plan 100% / Assistant Surgeon • 20% of the primary surgeon's eligible fee 100%

conclusion: Laboratory, Radiology/X-ray, Pre-admission Testing all covered at 100%.
exact_language: Laboratory 100% / Radiology / X-ray 100% / Pre-admission Testing 100%

conclusion: Prescriptions: 100% (no in-network vs. out-of-network split). 90-day supply max per prescription. If plan max is $1M-$8M, Rx sub-limit is $250K per Period of Coverage. If plan max is $20K or $100K, Rx limit is up to plan max.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Prescription Drugs and Medication
exact_language: Prescription Drugs and Medication 100% ... If the Certificate of Insurance Maximum Limit is $1,000,000, $2,000,000, $5,000,000 or $8,000,000 the Prescription Drugs and Medications Maximum Limit is up to $250,000 per Period of Coverage

conclusion: Physical Therapy covered at 100%. Requires medical order or treatment plan.
exact_language: Physical Therapy • Medical order or Treatment plan required 100%

conclusion: Home Nursing Care covered at 100%. Must be provided by Home Health Care Agency. Upon direct transfer from acute care facility.
exact_language: Home Nursing Care • Provided by a Home Health Care Agency 100% • Upon direct transfer from an acute care Facility

conclusion: Durable Medical Equipment covered at 100%.
exact_language: Durable Medical Equipment 100%

conclusion: Emergency Medical Evacuation: Up to the full Period of Coverage limit (vs. $1M cap in Patriot America Plus). Must be approved in advance and coordinated by the Company.
exact_language: Emergency Medical Evacuation • Up to the Period of Coverage limit • Approved in advance and coordinated by the Company 100%

conclusion: Teladoc Consultation included. NOT subject to Deductible or Coinsurance. Applicable in the United States. Note: Coverage for a Teladoc Consultation is not a determination that any specific condition discussed is covered — Company reserves right to decline future claims for conditions identified during Teladoc if otherwise excluded.
exact_language: Teladoc Consultation • Not subject to Deductible and Coinsurance • Applicable in the United States • Coverage for a Teladoc Consultation is not a determination that any specific condition discussed, raised or identified during such consultation is covered under this insurance.

conclusion: On Effective Date and renewal dates, insured must have legally departed Country of Residence (India for Indian nationals) and legally entered Destination Country (USA). Cannot have established Habitual Residency in Destination Country. Age thresholds: 65, 70, 80 for coverage tier reductions.
exact_language: (5) on the Effective Date and on subsequent renewal dates, must have legally departed the Country of Residence and legally entered the Destination Country (6) not have established a Habitual Residency in the Destination Country

conclusion: Urgent Care Clinic covered. NOT subject to Deductible. $25 copayment (waived if $0 Deductible selected). Plan pays 100%.
exact_language: Urgent Care Clinic • Not subject to Deductible • Copayment: $25 100% • Copayment is not applicable if the Declaration states a $0 Deductible
